titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 1293
GA 1293
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: 7.41 block marker moved yet again by David Parker.
7.41 block marker moved forwards two words. Then moved back two words by Rachel Kevern.
αυτ[ος] changed to αυτ[ον] at 9.21 by Rachel Kevern.
Error in supply tags at 15.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Word break in 18.4 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Illegible text replaced by uncertain and legible text and an editorial note regarding omitted end of verse added at 14.13 by Rachel Kevern. Supplied text added to 15.4 by Rachel Kevern.
Text of final four lines of F270v supplied by Rachel Kevern and David Parker.
Word breaks in 11.1 and 17.24 corrected by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Hugh Houghton.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number:1293Paris, Bibl. Nat.,Suppl. Gr. 1225Name of book transcribed: Four GospelsFirst folio transcribed: 221 rectoBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: WJETranscription begun:22-03-10Transcription finished: 14-04-10 Sam Gibson transcribed F 221v and 222r on 23.11.12. Images weren't available when the transcription was originally done.Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (>) - No Obeloi (†) - No Other remarks: The commentary text is a catena so does not interrupt the Biblical text which is therefore continuous. The links between the commentary and the Biblical text are a fascinating series of signs. 8,52 - 11,34 are misplaced later in the ms (commencing at folio 255recto) and then the ms continues on with 14,7, but the numbering is continuous over the misplaced pages. The end of the ms has been lost and it cuts off at 18,5 FINAL plain text transcription date: 24.04.2103

ab.note: folios 255r - 262v have been transcribed between F 246v and F 247r because that is their original place. They were bound incorrectly later and wrongly numbered.
The correction may be prima manu.
The end of this verse appears not to have been written.
